label: Discord — Community Chat Browser
description: Discord web app (React/Electron derived). Server list (left), channel list (mid), chat area (right). Login gate. Heavy JS SPA, lots of aria-labels and role attributes. Clobbered class names (minified CSS). 2FA (TOTP/SMS) very common — expect it. From ceki-comms session analysis: 103 mentions across marketing/comms agents. Second most automated platform after LinkedIn.
source_patterns: Server list click: 200-600ms hover → click → 1-3s channel panel load
Channel switch: click channel → 500-1500ms message history render
Message history scroll: upward infinite scroll for past messages, 300-800px per pull
Message composer: click contenteditable div → 200-500ms focus → type → Enter to send
Slash commands: type '/' → 300-800ms command menu → select → fill params
Reactions: hover message → click emoji button → pick from picker (1-2s)
Thread navigation: click thread → 1-2s thread panel slide-in → scroll → reply
Voice channel UI: click voice channel → 2-4s connecting state → voice panel
Login flow: email/phone → 800-1500ms → password → 600-1000ms → 2FA code input

active_hours.description: Community-driven — peaks afternoon, evening, late night

scroll.description: Fast chat history scroll, infinite scroll upward for history. Source: Discord web app patterns

platform_specific.text_fallback_strategy: Discord uses clobbered CSS class names (minified). When class selectors fail, use text-content matching: [...document.querySelectorAll('button')].find(e => e.textContent.includes('Log In'))?.click() — this is more reliable than class-based selectors.

login.two_factor_note: 2FA is VERY common on Discord (TOTP via authenticator app, SMS fallback). Expect 6-digit code prompt after password. If no 2FA prompt after 8s — account likely has no 2FA, proceed.

platform_specific.sequence_hint: navigate discord.com → login (email/phone → password → 2FA if prompted) → select server from guild list → select text channel → scroll up for history → read messages → type reply or use slash commands → react to messages → switch channels → optionally join voice
